MatExpSelectableButtonChange.description: Change event object emitted by button toggle.

properties.name: source
properties.description: The button that emits the event.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value assigned to the button.

MatExpButton.description: Directive to style the Angular Material Button component with latest Material 3 Design System Expressive styles.

properties.name: size
properties.description:
The size of the button.

Default: `s`

properties.name: shape
properties.description:
The shape of the button.

Default: `round`

properties.name: toggle
properties.description: The toggle state of the button.

MatExpIconButton.description: Directive to style the Angular Material Icon Button component with latest Material 3 Design System Expressive styles.

properties.name: appearance
properties.description:
The appearance of the icon button.

Default: `text`

properties.name: size
properties.description:
The size of the icon button.

Default: `s`

properties.name: shape
properties.description:
The shape of the icon button.

Default: `round`

properties.name: toggle
properties.description: The toggle state of the button.

properties.name: width
properties.description: The width of the icon button.

MAT_EXP_BUTTON_GROUP.description:
Injection token that can be used to reference instances of `MatExpButtonGroup`.
It serves as alternative token to the actual `MatExpButtonGroup` class which
could cause unnecessary retention of the class and its component metadata.

MAT_EXP_BUTTON_GROUP_VALUE_ACCESSOR.description:
Provider Expression that allows mat-exp-button-group to register as a ControlValueAccessor.
This allows it to support [(ngModel)] and reactive forms.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description:
The currently selected value. For `single-select` this is a single value;
for `multi-select` this is an array of values.

outputs.name: selectionChange
outputs.description: Emits when the selected value changes, either via user interaction or programmatic update.

MatExpButtonGroup.description:
Component that groups buttons and provides single/multi-select behavior
compatible with Angular reactive and template-driven forms.

properties.name: appearance
properties.description: The appearance of the buttons.

properties.name: size
properties.description:
The size of the button.

Default: `s`

properties.name: shape
properties.description:
The shape of the buttons.

Default: `round`

properties.name: selection
properties.description: The selection of the button group.

properties.name: variant
properties.description:
The variant of the button group.

Default: `standard`

properties.name: disabled
properties.description:
The disabled state of the button group.

Default: `false`

MatExpSplitButton.description: Component to style the Angular Material Button component with latest Material 3 Design System Expressive styles.

properties.name: size
properties.description:
The size of the button.

Default: `s`

properties.name: appearance
properties.description:
The label of the split button.

Default: `tonal`

toButtonGroupChild.description: Wraps a projected button/icon-button directive instance in its `ButtonGroupChild` adapter.

bindButtonGroupChildren.description:
Registers the effects that broadcast `size`/`shape`/`appearance`/`disabled` from a
group-like host (`MatExpButtonGroup`, `MatExpSplitButton`) down to its
projected `ButtonGroupChild` instances.

This is the single implementation of the broadcast-effect logic that was previously
duplicated between `button-group.ts` and `split-button.ts`. Must be called from within
an injection context (e.g. a component/directive constructor).

ButtonGroupChild.description:
Narrow contract that `MatExpButtonGroup` and `MatExpSplitButton` depend on
instead of reaching into the full public surface of `MatExpButton` /
`MatExpIconButton`.

Keeping this interface narrow means a change to either button directive's public setters
no longer ripples silently into both group/split-button implementations - only the adapter
for the affected directive needs to change.

ButtonAdapter.description: Adapts a `MatExpButton` directive instance to the `ButtonGroupChild` contract.

IconButtonAdapter.description: Adapts a `MatExpIconButton` directive instance to the `ButtonGroupChild` contract.

ButtonGroupChildBroadcastConfig.description: Reactive sources that can drive a broadcast to a set of `ButtonGroupChild` instances.

properties.name: children
properties.description: The current set of children to broadcast style/state changes to.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description:
Visual configuration of the indicator.

- `default` – renders just the morphing shape.
- `contained` – renders the morphing shape on top of a tonal background
  "container" circle.

inputs.name: ariaLabel
inputs.description: Accessible label announced by screen readers while the indicator is shown.

inputs.name: speed
inputs.description:
Speed preset for the shape morph and rotation animation. Each preset maps
to one of the M3 Expressive spatial spring tokens (`fast`, `default`,
`slow`) – see .

Changing this signal at runtime tears down the active GSAP timelines and
re-builds them with the new spring, so the indicator stays in sync with
the new motion language without recreating the component.

MatExpLoadingIndicator.description:
Material 3 Expressive loading indicator. Renders a continuously morphing and
rotating shape that loops through the seven canonical M3 shapes.

All animation work is driven by GSAP – there are no CSS keyframes:

- **Entry** – Angular's `(animate.enter)` event binding hands control to
  GSAP, which fades the wrapper from `autoAlpha: 0 / scale: 0.85 → 1` over
  ~200ms (`power2.out`) and calls `event.animationComplete()` once the
  tween finishes.
- **Exit** – `(animate.leave)` runs the inverse tween over ~150ms
  (`power2.in`); Angular waits for `animationComplete()` before detaching
  the element from the DOM.
- **Rotation & shape morph** – modelled on the Compose Material3
  `LoadingIndicator` choreography:
  - a continuous **linear background rotation** on a wrapper `<g>`
    (`intervalMs × ~7.178` per full revolution, matching Compose's ratio),
  - on top of it, a **per-step spring** that morphs the inner `<path>` to
    the next shape **and** kicks its rotation by 90°, eased through one of
    the M3 Expressive spatial spring cubic-bezier presets. The spring's
    overshoot is what produces the visible bounce at each morph boundary.

Motion tokens live in `loading-indicator.animation.ts` – the SCSS only
ships visual tokens (size, colour, ratio). Override motion globally via
`provideMatExpLoadingIndicatorOptions` or per-instance via the
`speed` input.

`prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is honoured on every layer: the entry /
exit tweens short-circuit to `event.animationComplete()`, and the
rotation / morph timelines are skipped via `gsap.matchMedia()`.

properties.name: config
properties.description:
The config of the loading indicator.

Default: `default`

properties.name: ariaLabel
properties.description:
The accessible label announced to assistive technologies when the loading
indicator is displayed.

Default: `Loading`

properties.name: speed
properties.description:
Speed preset for the shape morph and rotation animation. Each preset maps
to one of the M3 Expressive spatial spring tokens:

- `fast`    – Expressive fast spatial    – `cubic-bezier(0.42, 1.67, 0.21, 0.90)`, 350ms per morph step.
- `default` – Expressive default spatial – `cubic-bezier(0.38, 1.21, 0.22, 1.00)`, 500ms per morph step.
- `slow`    – Expressive slow spatial    – `cubic-bezier(0.39, 1.29, 0.35, 0.98)`, 650ms per morph step.

Default: `fast` – matches the original M3 spec for the loading indicator.

MatExpLoadingIndicatorSpeed.description:
Speed preset for the `mat-exp-loading-indicator`'s shape morph and
rotation animation. Each preset maps to one of the M3 Expressive spatial
spring tokens:

- `fast`    – Expressive fast spatial    – `cubic-bezier(0.42, 1.67, 0.21, 0.90)`, 350ms per morph step.
- `default` – Expressive default spatial – `cubic-bezier(0.38, 1.21, 0.22, 1.00)`, 500ms per morph step.
- `slow`    – Expressive slow spatial    – `cubic-bezier(0.39, 1.29, 0.35, 0.98)`, 650ms per morph step.
